definition: By surprise; startled.
I was completely taken aback by his sudden resignation.
in a way that is not normal
The weather was abnormally warm for this time of year.
side by side; up to date
The two runners ran abreast for the final hundred meters.
suddenly and unexpectedly
The car stopped abruptly.
in a way that relates to academic study
Academically, she was very strong in mathematics.
in a way that suggests one will not be persuaded
He adamantly refused to change his mind.
used to concede that something is true
Admittedly, I was wrong about that.
in a way that relates to the appreciation of beauty.
The garden was designed to be aesthetically pleasing.
from a distance
We could see the city lights from afar.
in a way that shows love or fondness
She smiled affectionately at her grandchild.
to or at a distance; away from home
His imagination often wanders far afield.
in a new or different way; again from the beginning
After the failure, they decided to start afresh.
at, near, or toward the stern of a ship or tail of an aircraft
The passengers were asked to move aft.
onto the ground or shore, stranded
The ship ran aground during the storm.
Without purpose or direction.
He wandered aimlessly through the streets.
in a way that causes sudden fear or anxiety
The number of cases has risen alarmingly in recent weeks.
sufficiently; more than enough
The evidence amply supports his claim.
in an anatomical manner; with respect to anatomy
The heart is anatomically designed to pump blood efficiently.
again in a new or different way
They decided to start their project anew.
apart
The two houses are far apart.
as far as one knows or can see
Apparently, it's going to rain tomorrow.
approximately
The journey will take approximately two hours.
in a manner that is appropriate or suitable in the circumstances
The new machine was aptly named 'The Quick Fix'.
in an architectural manner
The building is architecturally significant.
open to argument or debate
It is arguable that his decision was the best one.
in an artistic manner
She arranged the flowers artistically in the vase.
extremely surprising or impressive
The magician performed an astonishing trick.
away from the correct path or direction
The sheep went astray from the flock.
in a way that can be heard
He sighed audibly, showing his frustration.
later than should have been the case
He belatedly realized his mistake.
in an angry, hurt, or resentful way
She cried bitterly after hearing the news.
occurring or appearing every two weeks or twice a week
The team meets biweekly to discuss project progress.
in a state of extreme happiness
They were blissfully unaware of the danger.
in a direct, unreserved way
She told him bluntly that his idea was terrible.
in an active, quick, or energetic way
She walked briskly to catch the bus.
honestly or frankly
Candidly, I don't think that's a good idea.
In a charming or delightful manner.
She smiled charmingly at the audience before beginning her speech.
by chemical means; in a chemical way
The substance was chemically altered.
in a way that results from a coincidence
Coincidentally, we both arrived at the same time.
in a way that relates to commerce or business
The product was not commercially successful.
in a way that involves competition
He trains competitively for the marathon.
possibly; imaginably
Conceivably, we could finish the project by Friday if everyone works overtime.
In a way that is private or secret
He spoke to me confidentially about his concerns.
in a consecutive manner; one after another without interruption
The team won five games consecutively.
as a result; therefore
It rained heavily; consequently, the game was cancelled.
regularly, always
She consistently performs well in her exams.
in a clearly visible way
He conspicuously avoided eye contact with his boss.
happening repeatedly
The continual noise was very distracting.
according to accepted custom or practice
Conventionally, the bride wears white at a wedding.
in an opposite or contrasting way
Some people enjoy hot weather; conversely, others prefer cold.
in a way that causes someone to believe that something is true or valid
He argued his point very convincingly.
in a corresponding manner; accordingly
The demand increased, and prices rose correspondingly.
In a brave or courageous manner.
She courageously faced her fears and spoke in front of the large audience.
In a believable or plausible way.
The witness testified credibly about what she had seen.
in a way that is against the law
He was found criminally responsible for the damage.
usually; traditionally
They customarily meet for coffee on Tuesdays.
in a way that is good or acceptable
He behaved decently throughout the difficult situation.
misleadingly
The path looked deceptively easy, but it was very steep.
in a way that shows ability to make decisions quickly
She acted decisively to resolve the conflict.
in a manner that shows open resistance or bold disobedience
She defiantly refused to apologize for her actions.
on purpose; intentionally
He deliberately ignored my question.
in a way that relates to democracy
The decision was made democratically by the group.
in a way that is earned or merited
She deservedly won the award for her hard work.
in a way that is tactful and sensitive
She handled the difficult situation very diplomatically.
in a careful and prudent manner
She discreetly slipped the note into his hand.
in a way that causes disgust
The food was disgustingly sweet.
in a way that is characteristic of one person or thing, and so serves to distinguish it from others
The artist's style is distinctively modern.
in a way that is clearly heard, seen, or felt
I distinctly remember telling you that.
in a way that causes anxiety or uneasiness
The news was disturbingly quiet.
extremely; thoroughly (used for emphasis, typically to describe something bad)
His behavior was downright rude and unacceptable.
in the direction in which a stream or river flows
The boat drifted downstream with the current.
in the direction toward which the wind is blowing
The smoke drifted downwind from the campfire.
in a sudden and striking manner
The situation changed dramatically.
Extreme; severe.
The company took drastic measures to cut costs.
Extremely; very badly or unpleasantly.
She was dreadfully sorry for her mistake.
in a way that involves constant change or activity
The software dynamically adjusts to user preferences.
in a strange and frightening manner
The old house was eerily quiet.
in a way that produces a desired result
The new system effectively reduced costs.
without effort
She danced with effortless grace.
in a detailed and complicated manner
The plan was elaborately designed.
by means of electricity
The car is powered electrically.
in an emotional way
She reacted emotionally to the news.
in a forceful way
She emphatically denied the accusations.
completely; wholly
The situation is entirely different now.
basically; fundamentally
Essentially, we need to start over.
in an ethical manner
Companies should behave ethically towards their employees.
in a way that relates to a particular ethnic group
The neighborhood is ethnically diverse.
clearly; obviously
Evidently, he was not happy with the decision.
very well; superbly
She performed excellently in the play.
to an extent that is too much
He worries excessively about his grades.
only
This offer is available exclusively to our members.
in an experimental way
The new drug is being tested experimentally.
in a skillful or knowledgeable manner
She expertly repaired the broken watch.
by a very large amount; increasingly rapidly
The company's profits grew exponentially last year.
on the outside; outwardly
The building looks fine externally, but it needs work inside.
in a way that is barely perceptible or discernible
She could faintly hear music playing in the distance.
in a false or incorrect manner
He was falsely accused of the crime.
in a well-known way
He is famously known for his inventions.
positively
The critics reviewed the film favourably.
in a ferocious or intense manner
The wind blew fiercely during the storm.
appropriately; suitably
Fittingly, the celebration was held in the old town square.
perfectly
The dancer performed the routine flawlessly.
powerful, strong
Her argument was so forceful that everyone agreed.
previously
She formerly worked as a teacher before becoming a writer.
once every two weeks
The magazine is published fortnightly.
In a desperate or wild manner
She frantically searched for her lost keys.
in a way that relates to the way something works or operates
The old machine is still functionally sound.
in addition; besides
It's too expensive; furthermore, it's too small.
in abundance
There were toys galore at the children's party.
in a general or non-specific way
The term is used generically to refer to any type of plant.
in a generous way
He generously donated to the charity.
relating to genes or genetics
Some diseases are genetically inherited.
in a truly sincere or authentic way
He genuinely apologized for his mistake.
in a geographical sense; with regard to geography
The two cities are geographically close.
in a careful or cautious manner
He gingerly picked up the broken pieces of glass.
in a courteous and kind manner
She graciously accepted the award and thanked everyone.
in a grammatically correct way
The sentence was grammatically correct, but awkward.
without charge; free
The software was provided gratis to all students.
in a very serious, gloomy, or depressing manner
He grimly accepted the bad news.
in a way that is easy or convenient; with ease
The team won the game handily, scoring many points.
in a generous or ample manner
He was handsomely rewarded for his efforts.
in a way that is pleasant and agreeable
The different sections of the orchestra played harmoniously together.
with excessive speed or urgency; hurriedly
He hastily packed his bags and left.
With sincere feeling or enthusiasm.
She thanked him whole-heartedly for his help.
therefore, for this reason
The road was icy; hence, driving was dangerous.
in a heroic manner
He heroically saved the child from the burning building.
very, to a great degree
He is a highly respected scientist.
in a way that relates to history
Historically, this region was very important.
without hope; in a desperate way
He was hopelessly lost in the forest.
with great intensity or passion
The issue was hotly debated in parliament.
by human means or effort
They did everything humanly possible to save the patient.
by way of hypothesis; supposedly
Hypothetically, what would happen if we failed?
with uncontrolled emotion, especially laughter or crying
She laughed hysterically at the joke.
In an identical manner
The twins were dressed identically.
with no particular purpose, reason, or effort
He sat idly by the window, watching the rain.
to a great extent; extremely
She was immensely grateful for their help.
in a way that is not proper or correct
He was accused of improperly handling the funds.
not accurately; incorrectly
The report inaccurately described the events.
not sufficiently or suitably
The task was inadequately completed.
as a minor accompaniment or consequence
The costs were incidental to the main project.
indeed
It was a very good performance indeed.
without relying on others
She learned to work independently.
not directly
He indirectly hinted at his resignation.
not often; rarely
He infrequently visits his hometown now.
at first
Initially, I was hesitant to accept the offer.
towards or near the shore
The fishing boats stayed inshore due to the rough seas.
To an inadequate degree
The food was insufficiently cooked.
done on purpose; deliberate
His silence was intentional, meant to convey his disapproval.
at irregular intervals; not continuously or steadily
The rain fell intermittently throughout the day.
worldwide; globally
The company operates internationally.
in a private and personal way; closely
They knew each other intimately from their childhood.
by intuition; without conscious reasoning
She intuitively knew something was wrong.
In an opposite or contrary manner.
The two quantities are inversely proportional.
towards the inside
She turned her gaze inward, reflecting on her feelings.
in a way that is not based on or in accordance with reason or logic
He reacted irrationally to the news, shouting and pacing.
with another or others; together
They decided to work jointly on the project.
In a just or fair manner.
He was justly rewarded for his hard work.
in an eager or enthusiastic manner; intensely
She keenly observed every detail of the painting.
with knowledge or awareness
He went into the situation knowing the risks.
to a great extent; mostly
The success of the project was largely due to her efforts.
in a way that is permitted by law
The goods were lawfully imported.
according to law
You must be legally old enough to drive.
Lawfully; genuinely
He legitimately earned his promotion.
in a relaxed and unhurried way
They walked leisurely through the park.
along the length
Cut the fabric lengthwise down the middle.
in a literal or actual sense
I was literally shaking with fear.
in a magnetic manner
The particles were drawn magnetically towards the pole.
To a very great extent; very much (informal).
I was majorly disappointed when the concert was cancelled.
in a mechanical way; without thought or feeling
He performed the task mechanically, without much enthusiasm.
in a way that shows mercy; fortunately
Mercifully, no one was seriously injured in the accident.
In a way that shows great attention to detail; very carefully and precisely.
She meticulously checked every detail of the report before submitting it.
in a military manner or context
The country is militarily strong.
In a way that is very surprising or fortunate, as if by a miracle.
Miraculously, no one was seriously injured in the accident.
in a mocking manner
He smiled mockingly at her suggestion.
to a certain extent; not excessively
The weather was moderately warm, perfect for a walk.
lasting for only a moment; brief
There was a momentary pause before she continued speaking.
in addition; furthermore
The food was delicious; moreover, it was very affordable.
in a way that causes death; to an extreme degree
He was mortally wounded in the battle.
in a mutual or shared manner
They agreed to help each other mutually.
that is to say; specifically
There are two main issues, namely, cost and time.
as a necessary consequence
More money doesn't necessarily mean more happiness.
in a casually calm and relaxed manner
He nonchalantly shrugged off the criticism.
In a way that is easy to observe
Her health has noticeably improved.
in a formal and public way
The new building was officially opened today.
not on the stage; out of public view
The actor waited offstage for his cue.
at the site; on the premises
The technicians performed the repairs onsite.
in a continuing forward direction
From this point onwards, we will focus on the next phase.
in terms of practical operation
The new system is operationally sound.
by word of mouth; verbally
The instructions were given orally, not in writing.
in a way that is natural or not forced
The community grew organically over many years.
at first
The house was originally built in 1900.
completely, openly
He told an outright lie.
from a ship or boat into the water
Don't go overboard with the decorations.
excessively, too much
Don't be overly critical of yourself.
to a very great degree
The evidence was overwhelmingly in his favor.
in a seemingly absurd or self-contradictory way
Paradoxically, the more you try to control it, the less control you have.
to some extent
The door was partially open.
especially, specifically
I particularly enjoyed the dessert.
in a passive manner
He listened passively to the lecture, not taking any notes.
strangely, unusually
He behaved peculiarly after the incident, making everyone suspicious.
in a way that lasts or remains unchanged indefinitely
He moved permanently to Canada.
in a persistent manner
She persistently pursued her goals despite the setbacks.
in a political manner
The decision was politically motivated.
almost; in a practical way
It was practically impossible to finish on time.
exactly
That's precisely what I meant.
mainly; for the most part
The population is predominantly young.
mainly; for the most part
The audience was predominately made up of young people.
ideally, by preference
Please arrive on time, preferably a few minutes early.
before the due or usual time
The project was prematurely announced before all details were finalized.
immediately; quickly (especially as a musical direction)
He waved his hand, and presto, the rabbit disappeared.
by reasonable assumption
Presumably, he's still at work.
for the most part; mainly
The book is primarily for beginners.
in a way that produces a lot or a good result
She spent the afternoon working productively on her report.
gradually; in stages
The weather became progressively colder throughout the day.
in a way that corresponds in size or amount to something else
The reward will be distributed proportionally to each person's contribution.
in a way that corresponds in size or amount to something else
The costs will be shared proportionately among the members.
only, simply
His decision was purely based on emotion.
Having or showing a sense of purpose
She walked with a purposeful stride towards the finish line.
With a deliberate purpose; intentionally.
He walked purposefully towards the exit.
in a way that relates to quantity
The data was analyzed quantitatively to identify trends.
in a thorough or fundamental way
The new policy will radically change the system.
In a way that is based on or in accordance with reason or logic.
He tried to think rationally about the problem.
without difficulty or hesitation
She readily agreed to help.
fairly
The price was reasonably low.
without regard to; despite everything
He decided to go, regardless of the weather.
in a way that expresses regret or disappointment
Regrettably, we have to cancel the event.
in comparison to something else
The task was relatively easy.
Regularly and conscientiously
He religiously follows his morning routine.
reportedly
The suspect reportedly fled the scene.
respectively
John and Mary scored 90 and 85 points, respectively.
Having or characterized by rhythm.
The dancer's movements were rhythmical and graceful.
By right
The victory rightfully belongs to our team.
regularly, as a matter of routine
He routinely checks his email first thing in the morning.
in a royal manner; very much or extremely
The team royally messed up the last play of the game.
in a sarcastic manner; in a way that uses irony to mock or convey contempt
She replied sarcastically, 'Oh, how wonderful!'.
In a way that varies with the seasons.
The restaurant changes its menu seasonally to use fresh ingredients.
apparently, outwardly
The task was seemingly simple, but it proved difficult.
not often; rarely
She seldom visits her relatives.
in a way that involves careful choice
She selectively chose the best fruits from the basket.
in a sensible or practical manner
She decided to dress sensibly for the cold weather.
very seriously
He was severely criticized for his actions.
in a sexual way
The topic was discussed sexually.
In a way that causes shame or disgrace.
He shamefully admitted his mistake.
in a way that is large or important enough to have an effect
The cost has increased significantly.
in a similar way
The two cases were similarly complex.
at the same time
They arrived simultaneously at the finish line.
without assistance
She managed to build the entire house singlehandedly.
towards the sky
The rocket launched skyward with a roar.
only
He is solely responsible for the decision.
to some extent
I'm somewhat tired after the long journey.
to a severe or painful degree
The team sorely missed their injured captain.
toward the south
The birds flew southwards for the winter.
specifically
He asked specifically for your help.
in a way that relates to the human spirit or soul
He felt spiritually refreshed after the retreat.
directly; firmly; fairly
He looked me squarely in the eye.
in or to the United States
He's looking forward to going stateside for his vacation.
in terms of statistics
Statistically, you are more likely to be hit by lightning than win the lottery.
in a cautious and surreptitious manner
The cat stealthily approached the bird.
at a sharp angle; rapidly
The path climbed steeply up the mountain.
in a strict or severe manner
The teacher looked sternly at the misbehaving student.
immediately
Please come straightaway.
in a strict manner; rigorously
The rules are strictly enforced in this library.
in a structural way
The old bridge was structurally unsound and needed repairs.
afterwards
He failed the first test, but subsequently passed the retake.
in a subtle manner; not obviously
She subtly hinted that she wanted a raise.
in a brief and clearly expressed manner
He explained the complex theory succinctly.
enough
The food was sufficiently cooked.
in an appropriate or fitting manner
She was suitably dressed for the formal event.
according to what is generally believed
He supposedly won the lottery.
supremely (to the highest degree; extremely)
She was supremely confident in her abilities before the competition.
in a way that arouses suspicion; in a distrustful or wary manner
He looked suspiciously at the stranger.
in a way that relates to tactics or strategy
The team played tactically, focusing on defense.
in a way that shows good taste
The room was decorated very tastefully.
according to the facts or the exact meaning of something
Technically, the rule applies to everyone.
Near that place, time, or amount.
He lives in London or thereabouts.
in a thorough manner; completely
Please clean the room thoroughly.
as a result or consequence of this
He is ill, thus he cannot come to work.
without tiring; with great energy or effort
She worked tirelessly to complete the project on time.
in the end, finally
Ultimately, the decision is yours.
without opposition; with the agreement of all people involved
The committee voted unanimously to approve the proposal.
to an intolerable degree
The heat was unbearably hot today.
rarely or unusually
She is uncommonly talented at painting.
without being aware
He unconsciously tapped his foot during the meeting.
on the ground, typically beneath one's feet
Be careful, the path is slippery underfoot.
without doubt; certainly
She is undoubtedly the best candidate for the job.
in an unhappy manner
He unhappily accepted the news of his demotion.
in a uniform manner
The students were uniformly dressed in their school uniforms.
not done on purpose
He unintentionally deleted the file.
in a unique way
Each snowflake is uniquely formed.
by everyone; in every case
His work is universally praised.
in an unfair or undeserved manner
He was unjustly accused of the crime.
without being aware of something
She unknowingly walked into the wrong room.
in a way that cannot be mistaken; clearly
The voice on the phone was unmistakably his.
in a way that is not natural or normal
The dog barked unnaturally loud.
not officially or formally
Unofficially, the project has been approved.
as might have been expected; not surprisingly
Unsurprisingly, he won the race easily.
reluctantly; against one's will
She unwillingly agreed to help with the chores.
paid or given in advance; honest
You have to pay the rent upfront.
in the direction opposite to the flow of a river; at an earlier stage in a process
The salmon swim upstream to lay their eggs.
to a very great extent; immensely
The new system is vastly superior to the old one.
in a cruel or violent manner
The dog barked viciously at the stranger.
almost; nearly
The city was virtually empty during the holiday.
in a visual way; in terms of sight
The presentation was visually appealing with colorful charts.
in a way that is absolutely necessary or important
It is vitally important that you follow these instructions.
at all (used for emphasis after a negative statement)
There was no doubt whatsoever about his guilt.
after which; at which point
He finished his speech, whereupon the audience applauded.
in large quantities
They buy their products wholesale.
in a wicked or mischievous manner
She smiled wickedly as she planned her prank.
in a wise manner
She wisely chose to save her money.
